segment-text: Brian Duffield
THE EARTHQUAKE CONTINUES
NORAH is facedown on the metal grated floor, arms wrapped around her head. Eyes clenched shut. The sound of the quake is deafening, echoing in the tight
industrial hallway.
RODRIGO hugs a wall nearby.
NORAH! GET UP HERE! The metal bones of this place are screaming.
Condensation drips from the ceiling.
(quietly) I can't move. I can't move.
Rodrigo swallows a round of nausea as the quake keeps on.
COME ON, GIRL. THIS IS JUST AN AFTERSHOCK. Holy shit.
That... just makes me feel worse.
A huge cracking sound above. Rodrigo's eyes dart up in terror.
And then slowly... Slowly... The aftershock dissipates. Until the only sound left is dripping water.
That's a bad sound.
I'm gonna throw up.
I'll wait here.
He throws up. Norah rolls onto her back. A water drop hits her face. She sighs.
We're all dead.
Rodrigo vomits harder. THEY HELP EACH OTHER DOWN THE HALLWAY. Jules?
Rodrigo shakes his head. Mac?
A beam just...
He waves his arm down in a cutting motion.
Fuck. Aren't we supposed to be quake proof?
I think we are for normal bad quakes. That wasn't a normal quake.
Nope. It sure wasn't.
I've been in an eight before. I'm not convinced that aftershock wasn't an eight.
I'm not convinced that I'm not currently asleep in my bunk having the mother of all nightmares.
Rodrigo pinches Norah's arm. She winces and chokes out a laugh.
I hate you.
Trust me, I was really pulling to be a construct of your nightmare.

They turn a corner. At the end of the hall is A DOOR. Was a door. Now there's a fuckton of RUBBLE blocking what used to be a door. They both stop. Stare. Without looking at him, Norah pinches Rodrigo's arm hard. After a moment, he pinches his own arm even harder. THEY PULL AWAY THE RUBBLE. They shout to anyone that might be on the other side.
HEY! ANYONE OVER THERE!
ANYBODY! HELLO! No answer. So they keep digging.
At first I thought a group of you assholes snuck up behind me and started shaking my chair. And table.
And then it kicked up a notch.
She lifts a cinder brick that used to be wall aside.
When it did that, I thought, this is the end of the world. Me too.
I mean, fuck. I don't want to die doing paperwork on intake quantities. (MORE)
I'd much rather die with you in a hallway as we try to dig ourselves free.
That's the spirit. Thank you.

I was working out. And like, the weights didn't fall from their... shelves? Is that what those are called? It doesn't matter. But Norah, the weights launched. Like someone was throwing them. I was dodging 'em left and right, big fifty pound dumbbells and shit.
That's some security footage I'd pay top dollar to see.
They lift another piece away. Norah wipes sweat from her brow.
What if they left without us?
They're supposed to leave without us. That'sHello?
MUFFLED VOICE Rodrigo nearly bursts into tears. Norah jumps up and down happily.
Hey! Hey! We're over here! Who is that? It's Paul!
MUFFLED VOICE Norah and Rodrigo are kind of deflated.
(quietly to Rodrigo) Really?
(quietly to Norah) (loudly)
OH, NO RUSH! TAKE YOUR TIME!
You want to punch the sarcasm in Paul's voice. Norah rolls her eyes. The two dig fast. Her fingers are bloody but she doesn't notice or care. They whisper while they work.
He's such an asshole. Of course he survived. I can't wait for him to take the opportunity of our near death to touch my ass.
"Norah, there's only one way out of here... and it's through my disease ridden penis."
Norah almost drops a heavy chunk of debris by trying not to laugh.
See, you're a nice guy. You never say shit like that.
Because I have an immaculately clean, healthy penis. Ha ha.
Rodrigo smirks.
Gimme a hand with this one.
She gets on the other end of a five foot long concrete slab. They look up and see the massive hole above where it fell.
What is this from?
I meant-
NORAH
She points to the massive hole in the ceiling.
What's it from up there?
Don't think about it. Three, two, oneThey both strain and heave and slowly lift it.
Like jenga, the rest of the rubble falls away as they do so. DROP IT...
They let it go with a thunk. Light peers through the door. Rodrigo sighs with relief.
There was a part of me that thought water might come rushing in at us when we cleared that.
And what, Paul was just a... merman or something?
I put nothing past him. And, as annoying as he is, I will be very
happy to see his face. Rodrigo lifts up a beam a few inches so Norah can crawl through.
Yeah. Me too. God help me.
There is no God here. Only Paul.
Norah laughs as she crawls into-
THE ARENA.
Red emergency lights spin across the cracked cement ceiling. Norah is helped to her feet by PAUL.
What are you guys laughing about?
Hey Paul. Just, ya know, the state of the world.
That's currently not that funny.
No, I know, it's- Tiffany!
Norah runs away. Paul yells after her.
I'll just help Rodrigo alone! Rodrigo will be fine.

Norah runs past large rectangular WINDOWS that show... absolutely nothing but pitch blackness. So that's helpful. Norah runs over to the remaining THREE SURVIVORS. She hugs TIFFANY tightly. She is the smallest of the six.
I'm so glad you're alive. Me too.
Norah opens her eyes and seesNORAH Holy shit motherfucker.
That's exactly what I said.
Norah lets go of Tiffany and stares ahead at-
THE SMALL SUBMARINE.

The quake has pushed it from its conveyer belt platform. It rests on its side. Rests is the wrong word. More like it died on its side. Rubble from above has pummeled the tiny craft with a variety of dents and cracks.
We can't use it.
That's about it. Hey, kiddo.
He turns and hugs her quickly. CAPTAIN is in his forties. The affable demeanor of a high school football coach. His arm is broken. Bloody. Wrapped in a t-shirt sling.
You alright, Captain?
I mean, it's broken, but besides that, this is the worst day of my life.
Next to him is SMITH. He's a quiet one.
On a scale of one to ten, how bad is it, Smith? Ummm.
Smith looks at the sub.
So maybe... that's an eight.
And the other subs?
Captain points to the heavens and whistles.
At least some of the others made it.
Those pricks.
Rodrigo and Paul join them. Rodrigo sees the sub.
I was happier vomiting in the hallway.
You hurled?
Oh yeah. Not for the last time tonight. (beat) Unless I die first. So.
The escape pods are all gone too.
That's not true.
Okay. The escape pods that weren't damaged in the quake are all gone too. Better?
Could we fix any of them?
Everyone looks at Smith. This appears to be his area of
How much time we got?
Last aftershock was, what, five minutes ago?
Maybe more. So next could be... I actually have no idea what I'm talking about.
I read somewhere that aftershocks can hit years later.
Really? How's that work?
Who gives a fuck?
Good point.
Captain turns back to Smith.
Let's say you have fifteen minutes.
Smith thinks.
Can I have two days instead. An awful pause.
...No. And also there are six of us. So six pods.
There's only four pods left.
But they're all broken?
I'd need two days for one pod. Fuck.
Could we get to the other arena?
We could try, but I think we're almost out of time as it is.
You think she's dying?
Oh, she's long past dying.
Tiffany regards this.
Time on a good day to walk to the other arena would be, ten minutes? We've got no clue if the path is
clear, or if there's anything left
Plus, I was there.
They all look at Paul.
And now I'm here, so do the fucking math. They do.
And radios are down?
Captain nods.
There could be more of us here but... but we don't got time.
Everyone is quiet.
So... how do we get out of here?
Captain rubs his unbroken hand over his face.
I'm really sorry about this.
Norah puts her hand on Captain's shoulder.
It's not your fault.
That's not what I'm apologizing for.
They look at him. He sighs.
The Roebuck.
What about it?
We go there. It's our only chance.
Paul laughs. Sarcastically, obviously.
Oh wow, great idea, that's a really fucked up chance.
He's still the Captain, show some respect. (to Captain) How do we get there if there's no
subShe realizes. Stares at him. He stares back apologetically.
Oh fuck you.
THE SIX RUN THROUGH THE ARENA. Tiffany is limping, so Norah runs back to help her.
I can barely walk in here, how am I gonna do it out there?
Adrenaline?
Ugh. Maybe I can just cry my way to Roebuck.
I will happily join you.
The station GROANS again as it continues to slowly fall apart.
Sorry. Miserably join you.
That's the scariest thing I've ever heard.
Don't think about it.
What should I think about?
I don't know. Literally anything else. Fucking... McDonalds.

They reach the lockers along the wall that houseTHE DEEP SEA DIVING SUITS. Paul and Rodrigo carefully remove suit after suit from the locker. They are WHITE and look almost like ARMOR. Multi-jointed like an action figure, designed to withstand enormous amounts of pressure. The helmets are a fist-thick of glass all the way around. They are like a fishbowl, except for a large metal rim that run across it vertically on the sides. These are fixed with lights. And also, there are only six left.
Well. Hooray for perfect math.
Hooray. Here.
He hands her a skin tight wetsuit.

The six change as quickly as they can, considering their various injuries. Paul strips down to his underwear. Tiffany does not look his way as she pulls her suit up over her pants. Much to his disappointment.
You're putting it on over your pants?
Appears that way, doesn't it.
Won't it get all bunched up?
Bunched up pants are honestly the least of my concerns right now, Paul.
He shrugs and finishes suiting up. Tiffany turns to Norah. Whispers.
I don't have underwear on. Why not?
I was doing laundry when it hit, man.
Norah tries not to laugh.
But your pants will bunch up. Screw you.
Poor Paul. You have a great ass.
I know! That's why I'm keeping it to myself.
You should let everyone see it before we go outside.
Like a, good luck out there gang, and here's my ass.
Mission kicks off on a high note.
Everyone looks at CaptainWho is suited up only to his waist because of his broken arm. Even asking for help hurts his pride.
I need a hand. With... my hand.
Norah reaches into her wetsuit and takes off the belt on her shorts. Rodrigo eyes her.
It's not like I need it now.
When you get picked up by sailors, you'll wish you had a way to keep your shorts up.
When I get picked up by sailors, I won't want my shorts to stay up.
You're disgusting.
Norah smiles sadly. She holds the belt up to Captain's mouth.
And it'll be all your fault.

Captain takes some deep breaths. Bites the belt andRodrigo and Tiffany BEND Captain's arm grotesquely so it can squeeze into his suit. He, naturally, screams into leather. They get it in. He spits out the belt. Tears.
That wasn't even leather.
I'm sorry. They don't pay me enough to give a shit about belts.
I'll put that in my report. Holy hell.
I guess you can put your belt back on after all.
Norah tosses it aside.
Fuck it. I'm coming for them sailors.

Captain leans against the wall as Tiffany screws on his helmet. When the latches catch, the suit whirs to life, locking the rest into place on its own. Tiffany and Norah help each other get suited up. Paul and Smith wait on a metallic platform. Captain hobbles over.
Three rows of two?
Captain nods.
Rodrigo and you up top. Ladies middle. We got the rear.
Why am I at the back?
Captain does the smart thing (as he always does) and ignores him.
I'll start her up.
Norah puts her helmet on. The suit whirs to life. She walks past Rodrigo as he mans a control panel.
I hate this thing.
Yeah, but I love you.

Norah laughs at his odd remark. Winks at him through the helmet. Takes her place in line on the platform with the others. They click three inch thick platinum tether through belt holes built into their suits. Connecting the team one by one with separate lines. They help each other with long oxygen cords that screw into their backs. Again automated. The hiss of air when each locks in.
What happens if, ya know...
The station succumbs?
And we're still tied to the air?
Smith holds up his arm. A panel lights up. Eject.
How much air do we have on our own then? Honestly?
Or, what, fictionally?
Well, fictionally... Enough to make it.
Oh what a difference a word makes.
The platform starts to lower. Rodrigo still at the control station.
Let's go, Rod.
Just a second, boss.
He keeps working at the station. Tiffany grabs Norah's hand. They look at each other.
I'm a vegetarian. So?
So I've never even been to a fucking McDonalds.
They are about six feet down when aLARGE IRON ROOF begins sliding over them. Cutting them slowly off from Rodrigo. They all react.
Now!
CAPTAIN
Rodrigo walks over to the ledge and looks down at his friends.
Things I like less than earthquakes right now? Absolutely nothing.
He sighs and holds up his helmet. Cracked.
And IT IS. A long hairline runs across it.
Oh fuck me.
Rodrigo! Bring us back up now!
Captain tries to move over to the wall butThe link connecting him to everyone restricts him. He starts trying to unhook himself with his good hand.
Paul! Stop him now!
He'll fucking court marshal me!
Oh my God, do you realize this isn't the military? Worst he can do is fire you. So, stop him.
The ceiling keeps shutting.
G-go find another suit. We can wait for you down there.
That's a pretty lie.
The roof is halfway closed. It's getting dark. The crew's helmets automatically LIGHT UP their head beams. Captain tries to unhook again with his good hand. Paul reaches out to stop him.
I will fucking murder you.
Oh my God. Oh my God.
(to Rodrigo) I'm not ready to say goodbye to you.

Rodrigo leans so he can still see them as the roof closes. Raises up his hand to say goodbye. Norah raises one back andThe roof BOOMS shut, sealing them in thePRESSURIZATION CHAMBER. The six become five. More won't survive.
MOTHERFUCK! GOD!
Tiffany cries quietly. Their audio relaying into each other's helmets. Norah just stares at the space where Rodrigo was standing moments ago.
What will happen to him now?
Exactly what you think will happen.
WATER begins pouring in from TUBING along the walls.
I can't do this. I can't, ISMITH You don't get a choice about what you can or can't do anymore.
Tiffany understands. Starts trying to take deeper breaths.
Everyone's pressure good?
Everyone looks and nods. Water up to their waists. Smith checks a gauge and turns to his left. The crew follow.
Roebuck's little less than two miles on.
How long will that take?
Good day? 'Bout...
It's absolutely not a good day,
Smith mulls this over. Water up to their eyes.
It's gonna be a bad night at the bottom of the world, Paul. So shut the fuck up and get ready to walk for two miles. And we slowly fade out.

This is what you need to know about the bottom of the ocean in the middle of the night. It is nightmare dark. All of the sounds are muffled and awful. You can't run. You are walking with the weight of the entire world on your shoulders.
Your lights get you about ten feet ahead. The ocean floor is rock and sand.
And at the same time, you will never feel more claustrophobic

But this is what they are trained for. Today is just a really bad day. And slowly in the distanceTiny specks of light appear as the team of five emerge from the pressurization chamber. And into hell. The water is thick with SAND and DIRT churned up from the quake. There is nothing protecting them except their suits.
Everyone good?
You already asked.
I'm gonna ask every five minutes until we can see the sky.
But if we weren't doing good, we'd be dead.
...That's actually true.
And in silence for the moment, they walk on. They pass different support pillars for their station. Huge, thick arms of concrete and steel. Wide as God's bone.
See any cracks?
Norah looks up. Her light revealsA very big crack.
I feel like we should stop asking questions because the answers will always depress us.
Should we detach the oxygen?
What I just say?
I heard you. I just didn't care.
Don't unhook. Besides, if she goes down in the next few minutes, oxygen cords will be the least of our problems.
Smith looks back at Tiff.
Good job with the breathing.
Tiff nods.
Let's not talk about it. Let's just get to the Roebuck. Atta girl.
Here's what I'm thinking about. I'm thinking about how I'm going to have the easiest master's thesis of all time.
Norah and Captain chuckle. Smith even smiles.
And I'll be on the cover of Wired. And Time.
We'll be on the cover.
This is my fantasy. Norah can join me. The rest of you can settle for Newsweek. Ouch, kid.
Keep going, Tiff.
Okay... Book deal. At least two. The public have no idea how interested they're about to be in
ocean floor vegetation.
I still honestly have no idea what you do.
Because you are seriously the worst person alive at the bottom of the ocean. Oh boy.
Tiffany scores.
But even Paul laughs.
Fuck you guys. I'm sorry.
It's fine. Whatever. I know none of you like me. But here we are.
Here we are.
An olive branch, Paul. Your turn.
What I'm thinking of to get me through this bullshit? Yeah.
Paul laughs.
It's just going to make you dislike me more. Try us.
Come onnnn.
Fine. I really wanna get laid.
They laugh hard.
I think I started ovulating as soon as the quake ended.
Oh my God me too.
Smith raises his hand in agreement. Laughter.
Like I have never been more horny than I am right now.
It's your body's way of wanting you to reproduce inPAUL It's my body's way of saying, suck my dick, death.
Now all I see is the Grim Reaper on his knees in front of Paul. Somehow the most terrifying thing I've seen tonight.
They all laugh.
Norah sighs.
Stars, man. I just wanna lay on the grass and stare at the stars and name them and just, fucking, thank them for being stars. They agree.
You ever get cabin fever?
Oh yeah. But I mean, a job's a job. (laughs) I have a night light. No shit.

Projects stars on my ceiling. When I'd get really bad, ya know, the itch to not be cooped up in the station, I'd just go to my room, play some nature noises like rain and wind and just pray to God I could buy the lie for a whiThe ground suddenly RUMBLES.
Fuck fuck fuckHold on...
But there is nothing to hold on to but each other.
The rumbling subsides just as quickly as it started. From above, tiny bits of plaster float down in front of them. They watch it sink as they regain their balance in the dark.
Don't pay attention to it. Let's go.
He continues walking. The cord tethering him to everyone else tightens until Norah takes the next cautious step forward.
Mind your step. What-

Norah shines her headbeam down andTHE GIANT DRILLING HOLE LOOMS AHEAD. Too dark to see too far down it anyway, but rest assured, that fucker goes deep. A thick metal fence surrounds the hole, but is damaged here and there.
Drill's gone.
Norah looks up andThe iron spine of the drill has shattered. The drill itself nowhere to be seen.
Don't get too close to the hole.
Underwater drilling one oh one.
Nothing's leaking though.
Mmm. That might not be true.
I don't feel the need to find out.
Yeah. Whatever.
Norah swivels back towards Captain.
I like the new you, sir.
The one who says "whatever" when his multi-billion dollar job becomes an unmitigated disaster?
That's the one.
I mean, I preferred the old me for a few different reasons, but I see what you're saying.
What are you thinking about?
Oh, man. Camping. I really want to go camping. Go to REI, buy a nice tent. Go out into the woods with the wife. Campfire. Whiskey. (then, quickly) Sorry, Smith.
Why sorry?
Don't worry about it.
YOU'RE MARRIED?! What?
You said go camping with your wife? You're married! Kind of?
What does that even mean!
This is the highlight of my day.
You really didn't know he was married? He wears a wedding ring.
No he doesn't!
I don't, actually. You don't?
Norah keeps laughing.
Look, it's not really your concern.
Fuck that! It is so my concern! We have two miles to go!
Like, fifty feet less. You're married?!
Separated. Ish. Not divorced. Not... the fullest extent of marriage I guess.
No, I gathered that since you run a mining station on the ocean's floor.
Plenty of the crew had healthy marriages.
Shut up, Paul. Dude. Dude. This is blowing my mind.
You never asked. Really?!
I have known this about him for the entire time I've know him.
Is anyone else married? What else don't I know?
A pause.
I have a son. WHAT?!
Norah bursts out laughing.
I'm just kidding.

The group laugh and march slowly on through the dark whenAbove Paul at the rear- A solitary spiked LEG of what must be a HORRIFICALLY LARGE CREATURE about twenty feet tall comes into our view. Hovers over Paul for a moment. Before sliding down like a knife through butter. And in this case, Paul is the butter. He never felt a thing. He DIES instantly. His suit DECOMPRESSES in a flash, crumpling and expelling its oxygen outward. The others fall forward asThe Creature swiftly pulls its leg free and retreats back into the darkness. Spooked by whatever it just touched. NO ONE else has seen it. Yet. Ugh.
Everyone alright?!
They slowly clamber back onto their feet whenCaptain yells.
DON'T! DON'T TURN AROUND! Why not?!
...Paul's gone.
Smith disobeys and turns around. The girls follow suit andShine their lights on the fragments of suit and Paul floating down to the sand bed.
Holy shit holy shit holy shitShe turns away. Norah and Smith stare in horror. I...
God in heaven. What else is there to say?
They watch the pieces hit the ocean floor. Look at each other as their day somehow gets worse. Captain clears his throat. Forces himself to take charge.
Nor... Norah. Can you... help me detach this?
He holds up his tether, still connected to a piece of Paul's suit. Norah moves towards him and begins helping. They work in silence for a moment. Tiffany turns away, Smith's arm on her shoulder.
Did... was something... go wrong with his suit?
I guess. The poor bastardNo.
They look at Smith, who is still surveying the damage.
It wasn't his suit. It wouldn't have broken like that. Even if it had a leak.
Then what the fuck happened?
Smith is silent. Trying to make sense of it.
I think something hit him. More silence.
Like, debris?
There's nothing here, Smith! How much force would it takeSMITH A sizable amount of force. I don't understand.
They unhook Paul. Look at each other.
Let's get out of here. We can eulogize on the way. They hate to do it, but they move on.
Captain letting Paul's tether go. It floats there for a moment, as if he never let it go.
He was good at his job. He was.
He treated me nicer than... ya know. Others.
Last thing I said to him was to shut up.
Don't be hard on yourself about that.
Oh God. What are we going toNorah SCREAMS!
They look up asTHE EIGHT LEGGED CRAB WATCHES THEM. Standing calmly on the ocean floor.
Its shell is practically ARMORED.

It just stands there, staring down at the tiny people below. They don't move. It doesn't move. Without any warningIt skitters away in the opposite direction, vanishing in the black. And then they freak out.
THE FUCK WAS THAT THING?! Smith?
I have no idea. God that was big.
I've never even heard of anything like thatSMITH The quake must've... I really don't know. I'm sorry.
I think it's gone.
How can you tell!? It's, it's so fucking dark.
She begins to cry.
We're gonna die. We're all gonne die.
Someone slap her. Norah!
Norah reaches out and grabs Tiffany's arm.
We. Can't. Do. This. We can't. We have to keep walking. One step at a time. It's all there is to do.
I can't. INORAH If we don't keep moving, we will all die. That's a fact. And we're not leaving you behind. So don't kill us, alright.
Tiffany gets it. Nods. And starts limping forward.
Good girl.
He forces an encouraging smile.
You ladies keep an eye on the sides. I'll look backwards.

Norah and Tiff silently agree. Shift their bodies just enough to keep an eye on the flanks. And so they walk on. Captain reaches back a hand and takes hold of Norah's. Tiffany reaches forward and takes hold of Smith's. Norah stares straight up andIt's oddly disorienting. It feels like she's looking down a hole, as there is just nothing but pitch black above her. They are hours beneath the surface. Norah brings her eyes down and shakes her head. Her lights follow her movements.
You okay, Norah?
It's weird that you can get vertigo while looking up. But to answer your question, I'm terrible.
I know. Just keep your eyes on the prize.
Up is the prize.
The Roebuck is the playoffs. Up is the Super Bowl.
Sports! Can't even avoid them down here.
You love basketball.
She sniffs. Trying her best.
I'm pretty sure I am the highest scoring woman in underwater basketball. In human history. Damn.
I can barely see the station anymore.
There's a metaphor in there. (to Captain) How's your arm?
Still broke.
If it makes you feel any better, I'd totally break my arm if it meant getting out of here.
It doesn't, really. I'd break your arm too.
A little bit of laughter.
Not with that arm you won't. That's a one sided fight.
How do we... how do we fight? Ya know... that thing?
A long pause as they think it out.
Verbal abuse.
Stop joking.
Today's the first time anyone has ever had to ask him that.
I don't think he's joking.
I'm not joking.
"Verbal abuse" is not a joke?
Well when you put it like that.
So we're defenseless?
We can punch too. Nnnnnope.
What about lights? Can we blind them? Them?
I'm just being pessimistic.
I don't think pessimism exists where we are. You'd need neutral to have pessimism. We don't have that. We have nothing but... bullshit.

Lights could work, but I didn't see eyes on that... bug? If it, or they, are from... even deeper than where we are now, they might not even have eyes. And if they did, even our lights right now are brighter than anything they've ever seen before. So I don't know if brightening them would make much of a difference.
A painful pause as they realize they are basically totally defenseless.
That's the most I've ever heard you say.
Norah and Tiffany smile.
Know any jokes?
He thinks about it.
What did the diver say to the shark?
I don't know, what?
Smith SCREAMS. Everyone jumps and yelps untilThey realize that was Smith's joke. No one laughs.
That's so fucked up.
Are you serious, Smith?
...I'm not the funniest guy.
No. No, you are not.
It's kinda funny, maybe?
To who, Smith? ...Sharks.
Another quiet pause.
And then they can't help but chuckle. But only just.
Oh you're a son of a bitch. I know.
Any other jokes? Beat.
There are monsters now. That's a pretty great joke.
I hate dry comedy.
...It's a wet comedy.
Everyone groans. Tiffany cackles.
Oh man. I'm not even sorry. Fuck all y'all.
You shouldn't be. If we get out of this, I'm getting that framed.
When. Not if.
I'll send you all one. Not even the cheap frames. I'll get sunresistant glass and everything.
Make it water proof.
So I can put it up in the shower.
I'm never taking a shower again.
That's gross.
Fine. Never taking a bath again.
That's fair.

I mean, I'm intentionally going to move to a place with no tub. Or pool. Or... fucking rain. (beat) I also will need to change careers. (then) I would swear to never bathe again if it meant getting out of here, though. Sorry.
They have stopped walking. Specifically, Smith has stopped walking. He is squinting ahead. But there is nothing to see but the darkness.
Why have we stopped?
I thought I saw something.
Something like The Roebuck or something much more depressing?
Captain refuses to turn around and stare. His eyes always fixed on the way they came.
It was just a secThey see it.
It's a FAINT DOT, a far-off flash of blue. I see it.
What was it?
He takes a step forward.
I think it's a fish.
How can you tell?
Because working here is my job.
Then why are we stepping forward?
Because it could be the size of my fist. This sounds good to Tiffany.
I'm gonna punch it.
They walk towards it. How close?
Don't know.
Another (very minor) RUMBLE runs along the ground.
It's just an orgy of horse shit down here. Sharks!

Tiffany gasps asFour FRILLED SHARKS swim swiftly through the group. They are about six to ten feet long. They look fucking bizarre. Almost like swimming fossils. The group freeze, but Smith keeps walking.

Smith, listen to me. The more time you waste not doing this, the more risk you put us at. If there is another quake, Norah and I will die. That's a fact, okay?
Smith takes a deep breath, coughs grotesquelyAnd nods.
Kay. Don't die.
We won't. Ready?

He closes his eyes and coughs harder. Leans forward and begins working the panels. Norah puts a hand on Tiffany's shoulderAnd she steps away from the pod entrance asIt shuts. It's quiet for a moment beforeThey hear the whoosh! of the pod launching. Tiffany laughs, her eyes full of tears, her hands covering her mouth. Norah wraps her arm around her friend's shoulder.
He got out. He did.
We did it. I can't believe it.
Me neither.
Together, they look down the row of the remaining pods.
Fuck yeah.
NORAH
They run to door eight. Empty. Empty. They squeal with joy. Run to eleven! EMPTY. Is fucking EMPTY.

There is ONLY ONE POD LEFT. Their smiles slowly fade as they catch their breaths. Realizing: ONE OF THEM IS STAYING HERE. They look at each other. Don't say anything for a long moment. Norah almost laughs.
TIFFANY
So that's my line. Norah-
Are you in love with Smith? What?

She asks this, because Norah has RAISED HER FISTS.

By fighting me?!

Another AFTERSHOCK hits the station. They both struggle to keep their balance. Norah pleads with her.
Tiffany! Go!

So she raises her fists. And so they fight. It's relatively one sided as Norah slams Tiffany against the wallAnd PUNCHES her-
HardAgain and again in the stomach.
Tiffany grips Norah's hair and pulls her head back. Norah gasps but keeps punching. It is brutal. And selfless.
I'm sorry. I'm so sorry.

Tiffany reaches for Norah's eyesHalf hearted and without much strengthTiffany falls to her kneesStruggling to breathe. Norah grabs her roughly andCryingPushes her into the escape pod. Tiffany tries to resist.

I know you and Smith have a thing. He's going to need you. I know you have parents and a brother and your parent's dog is named Megabyte. I know you are kind and good and you have books to write and covers to be on, and I... I don't have that. I don't even know if there's a fucking world left up there after all of this, but I know that no matter what happened, the world is better off with you in it. So I'm going to shut this pod, and you're going to get out of here and I can feel so good that I helped my friends. That's all I want right now. Deal?
Tiffany is crying. Before she can answer-

Norah STARTS THE ESCAPE POD andRemoves her arms before the door closes. Tiffany criesTIFFANY I'll send help! Norah smiles and nods asThe door shuts. And it is suddenly very, very quiet. As Norah stands in the vast arena, all alone. She leans on the wall. Listening intently. It's quiet... Quiet... Quiet... Until suddenly, finallyThe whoosh! of the pod ejecting. Heading to the surface. And the air. And safety. Norah closes her eyes and her tears run down the wall. NORAH MAKES HER WAY THROUGH THE ROEBUCK. She limps and moves slowly. The events of the night finally taking their toll. A huge CAVE-IN blocks her off from an entire section of the station. She sighs and turns around. Limping onwards. SHE REACHES THE CONTROL DECK. A room encased in glass, showing the dark world outside. The red alarm light rotating. She stands on a chair andBreaks the light, so the room is a cool, calm blue. She moves over to a command chair and findsA CIGAR waiting there.
Oh worrrrd. She scoops it upLooks around the room. And exhales.

She chuckles and sits in the command chair. Puts her bare feet up on a counter. Sticks the cigar in her mouth. Wraps her hands around the back of her head. A DROP OF WATER hits her face from the ceiling. She doesn't even bother wiping it off asShe simply stares out the window. Into the dark. The swirling sand and mites and phosphorescent lights.
